Интеграция этого модуля в монокулярное Gaussian Splatting позволяет эффективно восстанавливать трёхмерную геометрию сцены в реальном времени с одной видеокамеры. Дополнительно используются техники динамического подавления и кросс-инференс выравнивания параметров камеры для повышения стабильности отслеживания позы. Экспериментальные результаты показывают значительное улучшение точности оценки позы и реконструкции сцены по сравнению с существующими методами.

en.title: M^3: Enhancing Pose Estimation with Multi-view Models and SLAM
en.desc: This paper introduces M^3, a novel approach that combines a Multi-view foundation model with a Matching head and Monocular Gaussian Splatting SLAM to enhance pose estimation and scene reconstruction. The Matching head improves the accuracy of pixel-level correspondences, which are crucial for precise geometric optimization in dynamic environments. By integrating dynamic area suppression and cross-inference intrinsic alignment, M^3 achieves greater tracking stability. Experimental results show that M^3 significantly outperforms existing methods, reducing average translation error and improving scene reconstruction quality.

zh.title: 提升姿态估计与场景重建的M^3模型
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种名为M^3的多视角基础模型，结合了匹配头和单目高斯点云SLAM，以提高姿态估计和场景重建的准确性。该模型通过细致的密集对应关系，解决了传统多视角模型在几何优化中精度不足的问题。M^3还通过动态区域抑制和交叉推理内在对齐，增强了跟踪的稳定性。实验结果表明，M^3在姿态估计和场景重建方面的表现优于现有方法，显著降低了绝对轨迹误差。

papers.title: Reliable Reasoning in SVG-LLMs via Multi-Task Multi-Reward Reinforcement Learning
papers.abstract: Abstract CTRL-S framework enhances SVG generation through chain-of-thought reasoning and multi-reward optimization, achieving better structural coherence and visual fidelity.  					AI-generated summary With the rapid advancement of vision-language models, an increasing number of studies have explored their potential for SVG generation tasks. Although existing approaches improve performance by constructing large-scale SVG datasets and introducing SVG-specific tokens, they still suffer from limited generalization, redundant paths in code outputs, and a lack of explicit reasoning. In this work, we present CTRL-S (Chain-of-Thought Reinforcement Learning for SVG), a unified framework that introduces a chain-of-thought mechanism to explicitly expose the model's reasoning process during SVG generation. To support this structured reasoning, we construct SVG-Sophia, a high-quality dataset containing 145K samples across SVG code refinement, Text-to-SVG, and Image-to-SVG tasks. By training the model to generate group-level structured SVG code, CTRL-S significantly improves structural coherence and visual fidelity. Furthermore, we adopt the GRPO algorithm and design a multi-reward optimization framework, incorporating DINO, image-text similarity, format, and code efficiency rewards. Through joint multi-reward optimization and multi-task training, our approach systematically enhances overall generation capabilities. Extensive experiments show that CTRL-S outperforms existing methods, achieving higher task success rates, superior SVG code quality, and exceptional visual fidelity.

ru.title: Цепочка рассуждений и множественные награды для совершенной генерации векторной графики
ru.desc: CTRL-S — это новая платформа для генерации SVG, которая использует механизм цепочки рассуждений для явного раскрытия процесса мышления модели во время создания векторной графики. Авторы построили высокачественный датасет SVG-Sophia с 145K образцами для различных задач преобразования. Фреймворк применяет алгоритм GRPO и мультивознаграждающую оптимизацию с использованием нескольких метрик оценки, включая качество кода и визуальную верность. Экспериментальные результаты демонстрируют превосходство CTRL-S над существующими методами в генерации структурированного SVG-кода и улучшении качества визуального вывода.

en.title: Enhancing SVG Generation with CTRL-S: Clear Reasoning and Multi-Reward Optimization
en.desc: The CTRL-S framework improves the generation of Scalable Vector Graphics (SVG) by using a chain-of-thought reasoning approach and a multi-reward optimization strategy. This method allows the model to clearly demonstrate its reasoning process, which enhances the quality and coherence of the generated SVG code. Additionally, the framework is supported by the SVG-Sophia dataset, which contains a diverse range of SVG tasks, helping to train the model effectively. As a result, CTRL-S achieves better performance in terms of task success rates and visual fidelity compared to previous methods.

zh.title: CTRL-S：提升SVG生成的智能推理与优化
zh.desc: CTRL-S框架通过链式思维推理和多重奖励优化，提升了SVG生成的结构一致性和视觉保真度。该方法引入了链式思维机制，明确展示了模型在SVG生成过程中的推理过程。为了支持这种结构化推理，我们构建了SVG-Sophia数据集，包含145K个样本，涵盖SVG代码优化、文本到SVG和图像到SVG任务。通过联合多重奖励优化和多任务训练，CTRL-S显著提高了生成能力，实验结果表明其在任务成功率和SVG代码质量上优于现有方法。

papers.title: Omnilingual MT: Machine Translation for 1,600 Languages
papers.abstract: Abstract Omnilingual Machine Translation system supports over 1,600 languages using specialized large language models that outperform larger baselines while enabling coherent translation in low-compute settings.  AI-generated summary High-quality machine translation (MT) can scale to hundreds of languages, setting a high bar for multilingual systems. However, compared to the world's 7,000 languages, current systems still offer only limited coverage: about 200 languages on the target side, and maybe a few hundreds more on the source side, supported due to cross-lingual transfer. And even these numbers have been hard to evaluate due to the lack of reliable benchmarks and metrics.   We present Omnilingual Machine Translation (OMT), the first MT system supporting more than 1,600 languages. This scale is enabled by a comprehensive data strategy that integrates large public multilingual corpora with newly created datasets, including manually curated MeDLEY bitext.   We explore two ways of specializing a Large Language model (LLM) for machine translation: as a decoder-only model (OMT-LLaMA) or as a module in an encoder-decoder architecture (OMT-NLLB). Notably, all our 1B to 8B parameter models match or exceed the MT performance of a 70B LLM baseline, revealing a clear specialization advantage and enabling strong translation quality in low-compute settings. Moreover, our evaluation of English-to-1,600 translations further shows that while baseline models can interpret undersupported languages, they frequently fail to generate them with meaningful fidelity; OMT-LLaMA models substantially expand the set of languages for which coherent generation is feasible. Additionally, OMT models improve in cross-lingual transfer, being close to solving the "understanding" part of the puzzle in MT for the 1,600 evaluated. Our leaderboard and main human-created evaluation datasets (BOUQuET and Met-BOUQuET) are dynamically evolving towards Omnilinguality and freely available.

ru.title: Специализация LLM для машинного перевода на 1600 языков
ru.desc: Представлена система Omnilingual Machine Translation (OMT), поддерживающая перевод более чем на 1600 языков с использованием специализированных больших языковых моделей. Авторы разработали комплексную стратегию сбора данных, объединив публичные многоязычные корпусы с новыми вручную подготовленными наборами данных. Система реализована в двух архитектурах: декодер-only модель на базе LLaMA и энкодер-декодер модель на основе NLLB, где модели размером 1-8 миллиардов параметров превосходят 70-миллиардный базовый LLM. Созданные модели значительно расширяют возможности качественного порождения текста на малоресурсных языках и улучшают межъязыковую трансферабельность знаний.

en.title: Breaking Language Barriers: Omnilingual Translation for All
en.desc: The Omnilingual Machine Translation (OMT) system introduces a groundbreaking approach to machine translation by supporting over 1,600 languages, significantly expanding the capabilities of existing multilingual systems. It utilizes specialized large language models (LLMs) that outperform larger models while maintaining efficiency in low-compute environments. The system's success is attributed to a robust data strategy that combines extensive multilingual corpora with newly developed datasets, enhancing translation quality across diverse languages. Additionally, OMT models demonstrate improved cross-lingual transfer, addressing the challenges of translating underrepresented languages with greater fidelity.

zh.title: 全语言翻译，打破语言壁垒！
zh.desc: 本文介绍了一种名为全语言机器翻译（OMT）的系统，支持超过1600种语言的翻译。该系统利用大型语言模型，通过综合的数据策略，结合公共多语言语料库和新创建的数据集，显著提高了翻译质量。OMT系统的模型在低计算环境下表现出色，能够在多种语言之间进行有效的翻译。研究表明，OMT模型在跨语言迁移方面表现优异，能够更好地理解和生成低支持语言的翻译。

papers.title: From Passive Observer to Active Critic: Reinforcement Learning Elicits Process Reasoning for Robotic Manipulation
papers.abstract: Abstract  Accurate process supervision remains a critical challenge for long-horizon robotic manipulation. A primary bottleneck is that current video MLLMs, trained primarily under a Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) paradigm, function as passive "Observers" that recognize ongoing events rather than evaluating the current state relative to the final task goal. In this paper, we introduce PRIMO R1 (Process Reasoning Induced Monitoring), a 7B framework that transforms video MLLMs into active "Critics". We leverage outcome-based Reinforcement Learning to incentivize explicit Chain-of-Thought generation for progress estimation. Furthermore, our architecture constructs a structured temporal input by explicitly anchoring the video sequence between initial and current state images. Supported by the proposed PRIMO Dataset and Benchmark, extensive experiments across diverse in-domain environments and out-of-domain real-world humanoid scenarios demonstrate that PRIMO R1 achieves state-of-the-art performance. Quantitatively, our 7B model achieves a 50% reduction in the mean absolute error of specialized reasoning baselines, demonstrating significant relative accuracy improvements over 72B-scale general MLLMs. Furthermore, PRIMO R1 exhibits strong zero-shot generalization on difficult failure detection tasks. We establish state-of-the-art performance on RoboFail benchmark with 67.0% accuracy, surpassing closed-source models like OpenAI o1 by 6.0%.
affiliations: The Chinese University of Hong Kong

ru.title: Критический взгляд робота: от наблюдателя к судье процесса
ru.desc: В статье представлена PRIMO R1 — нейросетевая модель мультимодального анализа видео размером 7 миллиардов параметров, которая превращает пассивные видеоанализаторы в активных критиков для оценки прогресса манипуляций робота. Модель обучается с помощью обучения с подкреплением на основе исходов, что побуждает систему генерировать цепочку рассуждений для оценки прогресса выполнения задач. Архитектура использует структурированный временной ввод, якорируя видеопоследовательность между начальным и текущим состояниями. Экспериментальные результаты показывают превосходную производительность как в контролируемых, так и в реальных сценариях, включая достижение 67% точности на бенчмарке RoboFail, превосходя модели OpenAI o1.

en.title: Transforming Observers into Active Critics for Robotic Supervision
en.desc: This paper addresses the challenge of effective supervision in robotic manipulation over long periods. It critiques existing video multi-modal large language models (MLLMs) that only observe events without assessing their relevance to task goals. The authors introduce PRIMO R1, a framework that uses Reinforcement Learning to enable these models to actively evaluate progress and generate reasoning chains. Their extensive experiments show that PRIMO R1 significantly outperforms previous models in accuracy and generalization, particularly in detecting failures in robotic tasks.

zh.title: 提升机器人操作的主动监督能力
zh.desc: 本论文提出了一种新的框架PRIMO R1，用于提高长时间机器人操作的过程监督能力。与传统的被动观察者不同，PRIMO R1通过强化学习将视频多模态学习模型转变为主动的批评者，能够更好地评估当前状态与最终任务目标之间的关系。该框架通过明确的思维链生成来估计进展，并构建了一个结构化的时间输入，以连接初始状态和当前状态的图像。实验结果表明，PRIMO R1在多种环境中表现出色，特别是在失败检测任务上实现了67.0%的准确率，超越了现有的闭源模型。

papers.title: SK-Adapter: Skeleton-Based Structural Control for Native 3D Generation
papers.abstract: Abstract SK-Adapter enables precise 3D structural control by treating skeletons as direct inputs through a lightweight adapter network that injects learnable tokens into frozen 3D generation models via cross-attention mechanisms.  					AI-generated summary Native 3D generative models have achieved remarkable fidelity and speed, yet they suffer from a critical limitation: inability to prescribe precise structural articulations, where precise structural control within the native 3D space remains underexplored. This paper proposes SK-Adapter, a simple and yet highly efficient and effective framework that unlocks precise skeletal manipulation for native 3D generation. Moving beyond text or image prompts, which can be ambiguous for precise structure, we treat the 3D skeleton as a first-class control signal. SK-Adapter is a lightweight structural adapter network that encodes joint coordinates and topology into learnable tokens, which are injected into the frozen 3D generation backbone via cross-attention. This smart design allows the model to not only effectively "attend" to specific 3D structural constraints but also preserve its original generative priors. To bridge the data gap, we contribute Objaverse-TMS dataset, a large-scale dataset of 24k text-mesh-skeleton pairs. Extensive experiments confirm that our method achieves robust structural control while preserving the geometry and texture quality of the foundation model, significantly outperforming existing baselines. Furthermore, we extend this capability to local 3D editing, enabling the region specific editing of existing assets with skeletal guidance, which is unattainable by previous methods. Project Page: https://sk-adapter.github.io/
affiliations: The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology

ru.title: Скелет как точный язык управления 3D генерацией
ru.desc: SK-Adapter — это лёгкая адаптерная сеть, которая позволяет точно контролировать структуру 3D моделей, используя скелеты как прямые входные сигналы. Метод кодирует координаты суставов и топологию в обучаемые токены, которые внедряются в замороженную базовую модель генерации через механизм кросс-внимания. Авторы создали большой датасет Objaverse-TMS с 24k парами текста, меша и скелета для обучения. SK-Adapter обеспечивает точный структурный контроль при сохранении геометрии и текстур исходной модели, а также позволяет редактировать отдельные области существующих 3D объектов.

en.title: Unlocking Precise 3D Control with SK-Adapter
en.desc: The paper introduces SK-Adapter, a novel framework that enhances 3D generative models by allowing precise control over structural articulations using skeletons as direct inputs. It employs a lightweight adapter network that transforms joint coordinates and topology into learnable tokens, which are integrated into existing 3D generation models through cross-attention mechanisms. This approach not only maintains the original generative capabilities of the model but also enables effective manipulation of 3D structures, addressing a significant limitation in current models. Additionally, the authors present the Objaverse-TMS dataset, which consists of 24,000 text-mesh-skeleton pairs, to support their method and demonstrate its superior performance in local 3D editing tasks.

zh.title: SK-Adapter：精确控制3D结构的新方法
zh.desc: SK-Adapter是一种轻量级的适配器网络，通过交叉注意机制将可学习的标记注入到冻结的3D生成模型中，从而实现精确的3D结构控制。该方法将3D骨架视为一种重要的控制信号，超越了传统的文本或图像提示，解决了精确结构控制的不足。SK-Adapter能够有效地处理特定的3D结构约束，同时保持原始生成模型的特性。通过构建Objaverse-TMS数据集，作者验证了该方法在结构控制和局部3D编辑方面的优越性，显著超越了现有的基准。

papers.title: Recursive Language Models Meet Uncertainty: The Surprising Effectiveness of Self-Reflective Program Search for Long Context
papers.abstract: Abstract Language models struggle with long-context handling, but a new framework called SRLM improves performance by incorporating uncertainty-aware self-reflection to guide programmatic context interaction, outperforming existing methods like RLM across various tasks and context lengths.  					AI-generated summary Long-context handling remains a core challenge for language models: even with extended context windows, models often fail to reliably extract, reason over, and use the information across long contexts. Recent works like Recursive Language Models (RLM) have approached this challenge by agentic way of decomposing long contexts into recursive sub-calls through programmatic interaction at inference. While promising, the success of RLM critically depends on how these context-interaction programs are selected, which has remained largely unexplored. In this paper, we study this problem and introduce SRLM, a framework that augments programmatic context interaction with uncertainty-aware Self-Reflection. SRLM leverages three intrinsic signals: self consistency, reasoning length, and verbalized confidence. These serve as complementary indicators of a model's internal uncertainty, and the model uses them to evaluate and compare candidate context-interaction programs. Extensive experiments across diverse benchmark datasets, context lengths, and backbone models, show that SRLM consistently outperforms state-of-the-art baselines, yielding up to 22% improvement over RLM under the same time budget. Our findings show that recursion itself is not the primary driver of performance in RLM, and a simple self-reflective program search can match or surpass RLM without requiring self-query or explicit recursion mechanisms. We find that for context lengths within the model's window, RLMs with recursion often degrade performance relative to the base model, whereas SRLM yields consistent gains across both short and long contexts. We also find that RLM is less effective in tasks with semantically intensive nature, where heuristic program search is insufficient and broader contextual understanding is required, while self-reflection in SRLM provides a semantic signal that better steers reasoning in these scenarios.

ru.title: Самосознание языковой модели как ключ к пониманию долгого контекста
ru.desc: В работе предложена система SRLM, которая решает проблему обработки длинного контекста в языковых моделях путём программного взаимодействия с контекстом, дополненного неопределённостью-осведомлённой саморефлексией. Система использует три внутренних сигнала: согласованность ответов, длину рассуждений и вербализованную уверенность модели для оценки и выбора оптимальных программ взаимодействия с контекстом. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что SRLM превосходит существующие методы, включая рекурсивные языковые модели, на 22% при одинаковом вычислительном бюджете. Подход демонстрирует особую эффективность на семантически сложных задачах, где саморефлексия обеспечивает лучшее направление процесса рассуждения по сравнению с простым поиском программ.

en.title: SRLM: Enhancing Long-Context Handling with Self-Reflection
en.desc: This paper introduces a new framework called SRLM that enhances how language models handle long contexts by using uncertainty-aware self-reflection. Unlike previous methods like Recursive Language Models (RLM), which rely on recursive sub-calls, SRLM evaluates context-interaction programs based on intrinsic signals of uncertainty. The framework shows significant improvements in performance, achieving up to 22% better results than RLM across various tasks and context lengths. The findings suggest that self-reflection is more effective than recursion for improving contextual understanding in language models.

zh.title: SRLM：提升长上下文处理的新框架
zh.desc: 语言模型在处理长上下文时面临挑战。SRLM框架通过引入不确定性感知的自我反思，改善了程序化上下文交互的性能。该框架利用自我一致性、推理长度和语言信心等信号，帮助模型评估和比较上下文交互程序。实验结果表明，SRLM在多种任务和上下文长度下均优于现有方法，提升了模型的整体表现。

papers.title: FlashSampling: Fast and Memory-Efficient Exact Sampling
papers.abstract: Abstract FlashSampling enables efficient categorical sampling by fusing the operation into the language model head matmul, eliminating memory overhead and reducing decoding time by up to 19%.  					AI-generated summary Sampling from a categorical distribution is mathematically simple, but in large-vocabulary decoding, it often triggers extra memory traffic and extra kernels after the LM head. We present FlashSampling, an exact sampling primitive that fuses sampling into the LM-head matmul and never materializes the logits tensor in HBM. The method is simple: compute logits tile-by-tile on chip, add Gumbel noise, keep only one maximizer per row and per vocabulary tile, and finish with a small reduction over tiles. The fused tiled kernel is exact because argmax decomposes over a partition; grouped variants for online and tensor-parallel settings are exact by hierarchical factorization of the categorical distribution. Across H100, H200, B200, and B300 GPUs, FlashSampling speeds up kernel-level decode workloads, and in end-to-end vLLM experiments, it reduces time per output token by up to 19% on the models we test. These results show that exact sampling, with no approximation, can be integrated into the matmul itself, turning a bandwidth-bound postprocessing step into a lightweight epilogue. Project Page: https://github.com/FlashSampling/FlashSampling.

ru.title: Семплирование внутри матрицы: от узкого места к молниеносной скорости
ru.desc: FlashSampling — это новый метод для эффективного категориального семплирования при декодировании языковых моделей. Вместо отдельного вычисления полного тензора логитов, метод встраивает семплирование прямо в операцию матричного умножения LM head, что исключает необходимость в дополнительной памяти. Алгоритм работает поблочно на чипе, добавляет шум Гумбеля и сохраняет только максимум для каждой строки, затем выполняет финальную редукцию. На практике FlashSampling сокращает время декодирования до 19% без какой-либо потери точности.

en.title: Speed Up Language Model Decoding with FlashSampling!
en.desc: FlashSampling is a novel technique that enhances categorical sampling in language models by integrating it directly into the matrix multiplication (matmul) operation of the model's head. This approach eliminates the need for additional memory usage and reduces decoding time significantly, achieving up to a 19% speedup. The method involves computing logits in smaller tiles, applying Gumbel noise, and selecting the maximum value efficiently without generating the full logits tensor. By optimizing the sampling process, FlashSampling transforms a traditionally bandwidth-heavy operation into a more efficient and streamlined step in the decoding pipeline.

zh.title: FlashSampling：高效的分类采样新方法
zh.desc: FlashSampling是一种高效的分类采样方法，它将采样操作与语言模型的矩阵乘法（matmul）融合在一起，从而消除了内存开销，并将解码时间减少了多达19%。该方法通过逐块计算logits，添加Gumbel噪声，并在每行和每个词汇块中仅保留一个最大值，最后对块进行小规模的归约。FlashSampling的融合核是精确的，因为argmax在分区上是可分解的，适用于在线和张量并行设置的分组变体通过分层因式分解分类分布实现精确性。实验结果表明，FlashSampling在多个GPU上加速了内核级解码工作负载，并在端到端的vLLM实验中显著提高了输出令牌的处理速度。

papers.title: MolmoB0T: Large-Scale Simulation Enables Zero-Shot Manipulation
papers.abstract: Abstract Zero-shot sim-to-real transfer is demonstrated for robotic manipulation using large-scale synthetic data and vision-language models with flow-matching action heads, achieving high success rates without real-world fine-tuning.  					AI-generated summary A prevailing view in robot learning is that simulation alone is not enough; effective sim-to-real transfer is widely believed to require at least some real-world data collection or task-specific fine-tuning to bridge the gap between simulated and physical environments. We challenge that assumption. With sufficiently large-scale and diverse simulated synthetic training data, we show that zero-shot transfer to the real world is not only possible, but effective for both static and mobile manipulation. We introduce MolmoBot-Engine, a fully open-source pipeline for procedural data generation across robots, tasks, and diverse simulated environments in MolmoSpaces. With it, we release MolmoBot-Data, a dataset of 1.8 million expert trajectories for articulated object manipulation and pick-and-place tasks. We train three policy classes: MolmoBot, a Molmo2-based multi-frame vision-language model with a flow-matching action head; MolmoBot-Pi0, which replicates the π_0 architecture to enable direct comparison; and MolmoBot-SPOC, a lightweight policy suitable for edge deployment and amenable to RL fine-tuning. We evaluate on two robotic platforms: the Franka FR3 for tabletop manipulation tasks and the Rainbow Robotics RB-Y1 mobile manipulator for door opening, drawer manipulation, cabinet interaction, and mobile pick-and-place. Without any real-world fine-tuning, our policies achieve zero-shot transfer to unseen objects and environments. On tabletop pick-and-place, MolmoBot achieves a success rate of 79.2% in real world evaluations across 4 settings, outperforming π_{0.5} at 39.2%. Our results demonstrate that procedural environment generation combined with diverse articulated assets can produce robust manipulation policies that generalize broadly to the real world. Technical Blog: https://allenai.org/blog/molmobot-robot-manipulation

ru.title: От симуляции к реальности: нулевая подгонка для манипуляторов
ru.desc: Исследователи разработали подход для обучения роботов манипуляции без использования данных из реального мира. Они создали MolmoBot-Engine — систему для процедурной генерации синтетических данных и собрали датасет из 1.8 миллионов траекторий экспертов для обучения политик управления роботом. На основе vision-language модели Molmo2 с flow-matching головой для предсказания действий были обучены три класса политик, которые успешно переносятся на реальные роботов без дополнительной подгонки. Результаты показали, что MolmoBot достигает 79.2% успеха при выполнении задач манипуляции в реальном мире, значительно превосходя существующие подходы.

en.title: Zero-Shot Transfer: Robots Learn to Manipulate Without Real-World Data!
en.desc: This paper presents a method for zero-shot sim-to-real transfer in robotic manipulation, showing that robots can effectively operate in the real world using only synthetic data. The authors introduce the MolmoBot-Engine, an open-source framework for generating diverse training data, which includes a dataset of 1.8 million expert trajectories for various manipulation tasks. They train multiple policy models, including a vision-language model with flow-matching action heads, achieving high success rates in real-world tasks without any fine-tuning. The results indicate that with sufficient synthetic training data, robots can generalize their learned skills to new, unseen environments and objects effectively.

zh.title: 零-shot转移：模拟与现实的无缝连接
zh.desc: 这篇论文展示了零-shot的模拟到现实转移在机器人操作中的应用，使用大规模合成数据和视觉-语言模型，成功率高且无需在现实世界中进行微调。研究挑战了传统观点，认为仅靠模拟数据无法有效转移到现实环境。通过足够大规模和多样化的合成训练数据，研究表明零-shot转移不仅可行，而且在静态和移动操作中都有效。论文还介绍了MolmoBot-Engine，这是一个开放源代码的程序数据生成管道，支持多种机器人和任务的操作。

papers.title: V-Co: A Closer Look at Visual Representation Alignment via Co-Denoising
papers.abstract: Abstract Pixel-space diffusion models can be enhanced through visual co-denoising techniques that incorporate pretrained visual features, with systematic analysis revealing key architectural and training components for improved performance.  AI-generated summary Pixel-space diffusion has recently re-emerged as a strong alternative to latent diffusion, enabling high-quality generation without pretrained autoencoders. However, standard pixel-space diffusion models receive relatively weak semantic supervision and are not explicitly designed to capture high-level visual structure. Recent representation-alignment methods (e.g., REPA) suggest that pretrained visual features can substantially improve diffusion training, and visual co-denoising has emerged as a promising direction for incorporating such features into the generative process. However, existing co-denoising approaches often entangle multiple design choices, making it unclear which design choices are truly essential. Therefore, we present V-Co, a systematic study of visual co-denoising in a unified JiT-based framework. This controlled setting allows us to isolate the ingredients that make visual co-denoising effective. Our study reveals four key ingredients for effective visual co-denoising. First, preserving feature-specific computation while enabling flexible cross-stream interaction motivates a fully dual-stream architecture. Second, effective classifier-free guidance (CFG) requires a structurally defined unconditional prediction. Third, stronger semantic supervision is best provided by a perceptual-drifting hybrid loss. Fourth, stable co-denoising further requires proper cross-stream calibration, which we realize through RMS-based feature rescaling. Together, these findings yield a simple recipe for visual co-denoising. Experiments on ImageNet-256 show that, at comparable model sizes, V-Co outperforms the underlying pixel-space diffusion baseline and strong prior pixel-diffusion methods while using fewer training epochs, offering practical guidance for future representation-aligned generative models.

ru.title: Визуальный со-денойзинг: рецепт для совершенной генерации изображений
ru.desc: В статье проводится систематическое исследование методов визуального со-денойзинга для улучшения диффузионных моделей в пиксельном пространстве. Авторы предлагают унифицированный фреймворк V-Co, который позволяет выделить ключевые компоненты, необходимые для эффективного со-денойзинга с использованием предобученных визуальных признаков. Исследование выявило четыре критически важных элемента: двухпоточная архитектура для гибкого взаимодействия потоков, правильно структурированное безусловное предсказание для классификационной guidance, гибридная перцептуальная функция потерь для более сильного семантического контроля и кросс-поточная калибровка через нормализацию RMS. Полученный метод показывает превосходные результаты на датасете ImageNet-256, превосходя базовый диффузионный подход при меньшем количестве эпох обучения.

en.title: Enhancing Pixel-Space Diffusion with Visual Co-Denoising
en.desc: This paper discusses how to improve pixel-space diffusion models by using visual co-denoising techniques that leverage pretrained visual features. The authors identify key components that enhance the performance of these models, including a dual-stream architecture and effective classifier-free guidance. They also propose a perceptual-drifting hybrid loss for better semantic supervision and emphasize the importance of cross-stream calibration for stable co-denoising. Their findings demonstrate that the new method, V-Co, outperforms existing models while requiring fewer training epochs, providing valuable insights for future generative models.

zh.title: 视觉共同去噪：提升扩散模型的新方法
zh.desc: 本文探讨了如何通过视觉共同去噪技术来增强像素空间扩散模型。研究表明，预训练的视觉特征可以显著改善扩散训练效果。我们提出了一种统一的JiT框架，系统分析了视觉共同去噪的关键组成部分。实验结果显示，V-Co在相似模型规模下优于传统的像素空间扩散基线，且训练周期更少，为未来的生成模型提供了实用指导。

papers.title: Mixture of Style Experts for Diverse Image Stylization
papers.abstract: Abstract StyleExpert uses a Mixture of Experts architecture with a unified style encoder to handle diverse semantic levels in image stylization, outperforming existing methods in preserving semantic and material details.  AI-generated summary Diffusion-based stylization has advanced significantly, yet existing methods are limited to color-driven transformations, neglecting complex semantics and material details.We introduce StyleExpert, a semantic-aware framework based on the Mixture of Experts (MoE). Our framework employs a unified style encoder, trained on our large-scale dataset of content-style-stylized triplets, to embed diverse styles into a consistent latent space. This embedding is then used to condition a similarity-aware gating mechanism, which dynamically routes styles to specialized experts within the MoE architecture. Leveraging this MoE architecture, our method adeptly handles diverse styles spanning multiple semantic levels, from shallow textures to deep semantics. Extensive experiments show that StyleExpert outperforms existing approaches in preserving semantics and material details, while generalizing to unseen styles. Our code and collected images are available at the project page: https://hh-lg.github.io/StyleExpert-Page/.

ru.title: Мудрость специалистов: семантически-осведомленная стилизация через Mixture of Experts
ru.desc: StyleExpert представляет собой фреймворк для стилизации изображений на основе архитектуры Mixture of Experts с единым энкодером стиля. Метод обучается на большом датасете триплетов контент-стиль-стилизованное изображение и использует механизм гейтирования для динамической маршрутизации стилей к специализированным экспертам. В отличие от существующих диффузионных подходов, ориентированных только на цветовые трансформации, StyleExpert сохраняет сложную семантику и материальные детали на разных уровнях абстракции. Эксперименты демонстрируют превосходство метода в сохранении семантики и обобщении на невидимые стили.

en.title: Mastering Image Stylization with StyleExpert
en.desc: StyleExpert is a machine learning framework that uses a Mixture of Experts (MoE) architecture to improve image stylization by focusing on both semantic and material details. It employs a unified style encoder that processes a large dataset of content-style-stylized triplets, allowing it to embed various styles into a consistent latent space. The framework features a similarity-aware gating mechanism that intelligently directs styles to specialized experts, enabling it to manage a wide range of styles from simple textures to complex semantics. As a result, StyleExpert not only preserves important details better than previous methods but also adapts effectively to new, unseen styles.

zh.title: 风格化的新突破：StyleExpert
zh.desc: StyleExpert 是一种基于专家混合架构的图像风格化框架，能够处理多样的语义层次。它使用统一的风格编码器，经过大规模数据集训练，将不同风格嵌入到一致的潜在空间中。该框架通过相似性感知的门控机制，动态地将风格路由到专门的专家，从而有效保留语义和材料细节。实验结果表明，StyleExpert 在风格化效果上优于现有方法，能够很好地处理未见过的风格。

papers.title: ViT-AdaLA: Adapting Vision Transformers with Linear Attention
papers.abstract: Abstract ViT-AdaLA adapts vision foundation models to linear attention Vision Transformers through attention alignment, feature alignment, and supervised fine-tuning to overcome quadratic complexity limitations.  					AI-generated summary Vision Transformers (ViTs) based vision foundation models (VFMs) have achieved remarkable performance across diverse vision tasks, but suffer from quadratic complexity that limits scalability to long sequences. Existing linear attention approaches for ViTs are typically trained from scratch, requiring substantial computational resources, while linearization-based methods developed for large language model decoders do not transfer well to ViTs. To address these challenges, we propose ViT-AdaLA, a novel framework for effectively adapting and transferring prior knowledge from VFMs to linear attention ViTs. ViT-AdaLA consists of three stages: attention alignment, feature alignment, and supervised fine-tuning. In the attention alignment stage, we align vanilla linear attention with the original softmax-based attention in each block to approximate the behavior of softmax attention. However, residual approximation errors inevitably accumulate across layers. We mitigate this by fine-tuning the linearized ViT to align its final-layer features with a frozen softmax VFM teacher. Finally, the adapted prior knowledge is transferred to downstream tasks through supervised fine-tuning. Extensive experiments on classification and segmentation tasks demonstrate the effectiveness and generality of ViT-AdaLA over various state-of-the-art linear attention counterpart.

ru.title: От квадратичной сложности к линейной: адаптация Vision Transformers через выравнивание
ru.desc: Статья представляет ViT-AdaLA — фреймворк для адаптации предобученных моделей зрения к Vision Transformers с линейным вниманием, решая проблему квадратичной сложности стандартного механизма внимания. Метод состоит из трёх этапов: выравнивание механизмов внимания через аппроксимацию softmax-внимания линейным вариантом, выравнивание признаков путём дистилляции знаний от замороженной учительской модели, и финальная настройка на целевых задачах. Подход успешно переносит знания из предобученных моделей вместо обучения с нуля, значительно снижая вычислительные затраты. Экспериментальные результаты на задачах классификации и сегментации демонстрируют эффективность и универсальность предложенного метода.

en.title: Transforming Vision Transformers for Efficient Attention
en.desc: ViT-AdaLA is a framework designed to adapt Vision Transformers (ViTs) to use linear attention, which helps reduce computational complexity when processing long sequences. It employs three main strategies: attention alignment, feature alignment, and supervised fine-tuning, to effectively transfer knowledge from traditional vision foundation models (VFMs) to linear attention ViTs. The attention alignment stage ensures that the linear attention mimics the behavior of the original softmax attention, while feature alignment helps reduce errors that accumulate through layers. Finally, the model is fine-tuned on specific tasks, demonstrating improved performance in classification and segmentation compared to existing linear attention methods.

zh.title: ViT-AdaLA：高效适应线性注意力的视觉变换器
zh.desc: ViT-AdaLA是一种新框架，旨在将视觉基础模型（VFM）有效地适应到线性注意力的视觉变换器（ViT）中。该方法通过注意力对齐、特征对齐和监督微调三个阶段来克服传统ViT在处理长序列时的二次复杂性限制。首先，在注意力对齐阶段，将线性注意力与原始的softmax注意力进行对齐，以近似softmax注意力的行为。最后，通过监督微调将适应后的知识转移到下游任务中，实验结果表明ViT-AdaLA在分类和分割任务上优于现有的线性注意力方法。

papers.title: Sparking Scientific Creativity via LLM-Driven Interdisciplinary Inspiration
papers.abstract: Abstract Idea-Catalyst is a framework that supports interdisciplinary research by identifying insights across domains to enhance creative reasoning in scientific discovery.  AI-generated summary Despite interdisciplinary research leading to larger and longer-term impact, most work remains confined to single-domain academic silos. Recent AI-based approaches to scientific discovery show promise for interdisciplinary research, but many prioritize rapidly designing experiments and solutions, bypassing the exploratory, collaborative reasoning processes that drive creative interdisciplinary breakthroughs. As a result, prior efforts largely prioritize automating scientific discovery rather than augmenting the reasoning processes that underlie scientific disruption. We present Idea-Catalyst, a novel framework that systematically identifies interdisciplinary insights to support creative reasoning in both humans and large language models. Starting from an abstract research goal, Idea-Catalyst is designed to assist the brainstorming stage, explicitly avoiding premature anchoring on specific solutions. The framework embodies key metacognitive features of interdisciplinary reasoning: (a) defining and assessing research goals, (b) awareness of a domain's opportunities and unresolved challenges, and (c) strategic exploration of interdisciplinary ideas based on impact potential. Concretely, Idea-Catalyst decomposes an abstract goal (e.g., improving human-AI collaboration) into core target-domain research questions that guide the analysis of progress and open challenges within that domain. These challenges are reformulated as domain-agnostic conceptual problems, enabling retrieval from external disciplines (e.g., Psychology, Sociology) that address analogous issues. By synthesizing and recontextualizing insights from these domains back into the target domain, Idea-Catalyst ranks source domains by their interdisciplinary potential. Empirically, this targeted integration improves average novelty by 21% and insightfulness by 16%, while remaining grounded in the original research problem.
affiliations: Siebel School of Computing and Data Science, University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign

ru.title: Наведение мостов между науками через систематический поиск трансферных инсайтов
ru.desc: Idea-Catalyst — это фреймворк для поддержки междисциплинарных исследований, который использует большие языковые модели для выявления инсайтов из различных областей науки. Система разлагает исследовательскую задачу на целевые вопросы, переформулирует возникающие проблемы в доменно-независимые концепции и извлекает релевантные решения из других дисциплин. Фреймворк реализует ключевые когнитивные механизмы междисциплинарного мышления: формулирование целей, анализ вызовов в домене и стратегический поиск трансферных идей с высоким потенциалом влияния. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что целенаправленная интеграция знаний из разных областей повышает оригинальность идей на 21% и их глубину анализа на 16%.

en.title: Unlocking Interdisciplinary Insights for Creative Discovery
en.desc: The paper introduces Idea-Catalyst, a framework designed to enhance interdisciplinary research by facilitating creative reasoning in scientific discovery. It addresses the limitation of existing AI approaches that focus on automating experiments rather than supporting collaborative exploration of ideas. Idea-Catalyst helps researchers break down abstract goals into specific research questions, allowing for the identification of relevant insights from various disciplines. By synthesizing these insights, the framework improves the novelty and insightfulness of research outcomes while maintaining a focus on the original problem.

zh.title: 跨学科研究的创新推理助推器
zh.desc: Idea-Catalyst是一个支持跨学科研究的框架，通过识别不同领域的见解来增强科学发现中的创造性推理。该框架旨在帮助头脑风暴阶段，避免过早集中于特定解决方案。它通过将抽象研究目标分解为核心研究问题，促进对进展和挑战的分析，并将这些挑战重新表述为领域无关的概念问题。通过整合来自心理学、社会学等外部学科的见解，Idea-Catalyst提高了研究的创新性和洞察力。

papers.title: Anticipatory Planning for Multimodal AI Agents
papers.abstract: Abstract TraceR1 is a two-stage reinforcement learning framework that enhances multimodal agents' planning capabilities through anticipatory trajectory reasoning and execution feedback refinement.  					AI-generated summary Recent advances in multimodal agents have improved computer-use interaction and tool-usage, yet most existing systems remain reactive, optimizing actions in isolation without reasoning about future states or long-term goals. This limits planning coherence and prevents agents from reliably solving high-level, multi-step tasks. We introduce TraceR1, a two-stage reinforcement learning framework that explicitly trains anticipatory reasoning by forecasting short-horizon trajectories before execution. The first stage performs trajectory-level reinforcement learning with rewards that enforce global consistency across predicted action sequences. The second stage applies grounded reinforcement fine-tuning, using execution feedback from frozen tool agents to refine step-level accuracy and executability. TraceR1 is evaluated across seven benchmarks, covering online computer-use, offline computer-use benchmarks, and multimodal tool-use reasoning tasks, where it achieves substantial improvements in planning stability, execution robustness, and generalization over reactive and single-stage baselines. These results show that anticipatory trajectory reasoning is a key principle for building multimodal agents that can reason, plan, and act effectively in complex real-world environments.
affiliations: State University of New York at Buffalo

ru.title: Упреждающее предсказание траекторий для планирования мультимодальных агентов
ru.desc: TraceR1 — это двухэтапная система обучения с подкреплением, которая улучшает возможности планирования мультимодальных агентов. На первом этапе модель обучается предсказывать короткие траектории будущих действий, получая награды за согласованность всей последовательности. На втором этапе система уточняется на основе обратной связи от реальных инструментов, повышая точность выполнения отдельных шагов. Метод продемонстрировал значительные улучшения в семи бенчмарках, показав, что упреждающее планирование траекторий — ключ к эффективной работе агентов в сложных реальных задачах.

en.title: Enhancing Multimodal Agents with Anticipatory Planning
en.desc: TraceR1 is a two-stage reinforcement learning framework designed to improve the planning abilities of multimodal agents. It focuses on anticipatory trajectory reasoning, allowing agents to predict future actions and outcomes before executing them. The first stage involves trajectory-level reinforcement learning to ensure that the predicted actions are consistent and coherent. The second stage fine-tunes these actions using feedback from execution, leading to better accuracy and reliability in complex tasks.

zh.title: 预期轨迹推理：提升多模态智能体的规划能力
zh.desc: TraceR1 是一个两阶段的强化学习框架，旨在通过预期轨迹推理和执行反馈优化多模态智能体的规划能力。现有的多模态系统大多是反应式的，缺乏对未来状态和长期目标的推理，限制了其规划的连贯性。TraceR1 的第一阶段通过轨迹级强化学习来训练预期推理，确保预测的动作序列在全局上的一致性。第二阶段则利用执行反馈进行精细调整，提高步骤级的准确性和可执行性，从而在复杂环境中有效地进行推理、规划和行动。

papers.title: Learning Human-Object Interaction for 3D Human Pose Estimation from LiDAR Point Clouds
papers.abstract: Abstract Human-Object Interaction Learning framework addresses challenges in 3D human pose estimation from LiDAR point clouds by mitigating spatial ambiguity and class imbalance through contrastive learning and adaptive pooling techniques.  					AI-generated summary Understanding humans from LiDAR point clouds is one of the most critical tasks in autonomous driving due to its close relationships with pedestrian safety, yet it remains challenging in the presence of diverse human-object interactions and cluttered backgrounds. Nevertheless, existing methods largely overlook the potential of leveraging human-object interactions to build robust 3D human pose estimation frameworks. There are two major challenges that motivate the incorporation of human-object interaction. First, human-object interactions introduce spatial ambiguity between human and object points, which often leads to erroneous 3D human keypoint predictions in interaction regions. Second, there exists severe class imbalance in the number of points between interacting and non-interacting body parts, with the interaction-frequent regions such as hand and foot being sparsely observed in LiDAR data. To address these challenges, we propose a Human-Object Interaction Learning (HOIL) framework for robust 3D human pose estimation from LiDAR point clouds. To mitigate the spatial ambiguity issue, we present human-object interaction-aware contrastive learning (HOICL) that effectively enhances feature discrimination between human and object points, particularly in interaction regions. To alleviate the class imbalance issue, we introduce contact-aware part-guided pooling (CPPool) that adaptively reallocates representational capacity by compressing overrepresented points while preserving informative points from interacting body parts. In addition, we present an optional contact-based temporal refinement that refines erroneous per-frame keypoint estimates using contact cues over time. As a result, our HOIL effectively leverages human-object interaction to resolve spatial ambiguity and class imbalance in interaction regions. Codes will be released.
affiliations: Dept. of ECE&ASRI, Seoul National University

ru.title: Взаимодействие человека и объектов для надёжной оценки позы в облаках точек
ru.desc: В работе предложена структура HOIL для оценки трёхмерной позы человека из облаков точек LiDAR, которая учитывает взаимодействие человека с объектами. Авторы решают проблему пространственной неоднозначности между точками человека и объекта с помощью контрастного обучения, чувствительного к взаимодействиям. Для борьбы с дисбалансом классов (редкие точки в областях рук и ног) используется адаптивное пулинг с учётом контакта, которое перераспределяет представительскую способность признаков. Дополнительно применяется временное уточнение на основе контактных сигналов между кадрами для повышения точности оценки позы.

en.title: Enhancing 3D Human Pose Estimation through Human-Object Interaction
en.desc: The Human-Object Interaction Learning (HOIL) framework improves 3D human pose estimation from LiDAR point clouds by addressing spatial ambiguity and class imbalance. It uses contrastive learning to enhance the distinction between human and object points, especially in areas where they interact. Additionally, it employs adaptive pooling techniques to manage the uneven distribution of data points, ensuring that important features from interacting body parts are preserved. This approach ultimately leads to more accurate predictions of human poses in complex environments, which is crucial for applications like autonomous driving.

zh.title: 人-物交互助力3D姿态估计
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种人-物交互学习框架，旨在解决从LiDAR点云中进行3D人类姿态估计的挑战。该框架通过对比学习和自适应池化技术，减轻了空间模糊性和类别不平衡的问题。具体来说，采用人-物交互感知的对比学习方法，提高了人类和物体点之间的特征区分能力。通过接触感知的部分引导池化，框架有效地重新分配了表示能力，从而改善了交互区域的关键点预测。

papers.title: Polyglot-Lion: Efficient Multilingual ASR for Singapore via Balanced Fine-Tuning of Qwen3-ASR
papers.abstract: Abstract Polyglot-Lion, a compact multilingual ASR model family for Singapore's linguistic diversity, achieves competitive performance with significantly reduced training cost and improved inference speed through balanced fine-tuning of pretrained models.  					AI-generated summary We present Polyglot-Lion, a family of compact multilingual automatic speech recognition (ASR) models tailored for the linguistic landscape of Singapore, covering English, Mandarin, Tamil, and Malay. Our models are obtained by fine-tuning Qwen3-ASR-0.6B and Qwen3-ASR-1.7B exclusively on publicly available speech corpora, using a balanced sampling strategy that equalizes the number of training utterances per language and deliberately omits language-tag conditioning so that the model learns to identify languages implicitly from audio. On 12 benchmarks spanning the four target languages, Polyglot-Lion-1.7B achieves an average error rate of 14.85, competitive with MERaLiON-2-10B-ASR (14.32) - a model 6x larger - while incurring a training cost of \81 on a single RTX PRO 6000 GPU compared to 18,862 for the 128-GPU baseline. Inference throughput is approximately 20x faster than MERaLiON at 0.10 s/sample versus 2.02 s/sample. These results demonstrate that linguistically balanced fine-tuning of moderate-scale pretrained models can yield deployment-ready multilingual ASR at a fraction of the cost of larger specialist systems.

ru.title: Компактные мультиязычные модели распознавания речи через сбалансированный fine-tuning
ru.desc: В статье представлена Polyglot-Lion — семейство компактных мультиязычных моделей распознавания речи (ASR), разработанных для многоязычного ландшафта Сингапура с поддержкой английского, мандарина, тамильского и малайского языков. Модели получены путём fine-tuning предобученных моделей Qwen3-ASR на открытых речевых корпусах с использованием сбалансированной стратегии выборки и без явного кодирования языка, позволяя модели неявно определять язык из аудиосигнала. Polyglot-Lion-1.7B достигает конкурентоспособной частоты ошибок (14.85) при 6-кратном уменьшении размера модели и 230-кратном снижении стоимости обучения по сравнению с базовой системой. Предложенный подход демонстрирует, что сбалансированный fine-tuning модерируемых предобученных моделей позволяет создавать эффективные мультиязычные ASR-системы с минимальными вычислительными затратами.

en.title: Efficient Multilingual ASR for Singapore's Linguistic Diversity
en.desc: The paper introduces Polyglot-Lion, a series of compact multilingual automatic speech recognition (ASR) models designed for Singapore's diverse languages, including English, Mandarin, Tamil, and Malay. These models are fine-tuned from existing pretrained models using a balanced sampling method that ensures equal representation of each language in the training data. The results show that Polyglot-Lion-1.7B achieves a competitive error rate while being significantly smaller and cheaper to train than larger models. Additionally, it offers much faster inference speeds, demonstrating the effectiveness of balanced fine-tuning for multilingual ASR applications.

zh.title: 平衡微调，低成本多语言识别
zh.desc: Polyglot-Lion是一种针对新加坡语言多样性的紧凑型多语言自动语音识别（ASR）模型系列。该模型通过对预训练模型进行平衡微调，显著降低了训练成本并提高了推理速度。模型在英语、普通话、泰米尔语和马来语等四种语言上表现出色，平均错误率为14.85，接近一个六倍更大的模型。研究表明，适度规模的预训练模型经过语言平衡微调后，可以以较低的成本实现多语言ASR的部署。

papers.title: OneWorld: Taming Scene Generation with 3D Unified Representation Autoencoder
papers.abstract: Abstract OneWorld enables 3D scene generation by performing diffusion in a unified 3D representation space using a 3D Unified Representation Autoencoder and specialized consistency losses.  					AI-generated summary Existing diffusion-based 3D scene generation methods primarily operate in 2D image/video latent spaces, which makes maintaining cross-view appearance and geometric consistency inherently challenging. To bridge this gap, we present OneWorld, a framework that performs diffusion directly within a coherent 3D representation space. Central to our approach is the 3D Unified Representation Autoencoder (3D-URAE); it leverages pretrained 3D foundation models and augments their geometry-centric nature by injecting appearance and distilling semantics into a unified 3D latent space. Furthermore, we introduce token-level Cross-View-Correspondence (CVC) consistency loss to explicitly enforce structural alignment across views, and propose Manifold-Drift Forcing (MDF) to mitigate train-inference exposure bias and shape a robust 3D manifold by mixing drifted and original representations. Comprehensive experiments demonstrate that OneWorld generates high-quality 3D scenes with superior cross-view consistency compared to state-of-the-art 2D-based methods. Our code will be available at https://github.com/SensenGao/OneWorld.
affiliations: Mohamed bin Zayed University of Artificial Intelligence

ru.title: Диффузия в едином 3D пространстве для согласованной генерации сцен
ru.desc: OneWorld представляет новый подход к генерации 3D сцен, выполняя диффузию прямо в единого 3D представлении вместо традиционных 2D латентных пространств. Ключевой компонент — 3D Unified Representation Autoencoder (3D-URAE), который объединяет претренированные 3D модели-основы и вводит информацию о внешнем виде и семантике в единое 3D латентное пространство. Авторы предлагают потоковую потерю Cross-View-Correspondence (CVC) для явного обеспечения согласованности между видами и Manifold-Drift Forcing (MDF) для уменьшения смещения экспозиции обучения-инференса. Эксперименты показывают, что метод генерирует высококачественные 3D сцены с превосходной межвидовой согласованностью в сравнении с современными 2D-методами.

en.title: OneWorld: Bridging 3D Scene Generation with Unified Diffusion
en.desc: OneWorld is a novel framework for generating 3D scenes by utilizing diffusion processes within a unified 3D representation space. It employs a 3D Unified Representation Autoencoder (3D-URAE) that enhances existing 3D models by integrating appearance and semantic information into a single latent space. The framework introduces a token-level Cross-View-Correspondence (CVC) consistency loss to ensure that different views of the scene maintain structural alignment. Additionally, it incorporates Manifold-Drift Forcing (MDF) to reduce biases during training and improve the robustness of the generated 3D representations.

zh.title: 统一3D表示，生成高质量场景
zh.desc: OneWorld 是一个用于生成 3D 场景的框架，它通过在统一的 3D 表示空间中进行扩散来实现。该方法使用了 3D 统一表示自编码器（3D-URAE），并结合了预训练的 3D 基础模型，以增强几何特性并注入外观信息。为了确保不同视角之间的结构一致性，OneWorld 引入了基于标记的跨视角对应一致性损失（CVC）。此外，提出的流形漂移强制（MDF）方法可以减少训练和推理之间的偏差，从而生成高质量且视角一致的 3D 场景。

papers.title: MDM-Prime-v2: Binary Encoding and Index Shuffling Enable Compute-optimal Scaling of Diffusion Language Models
papers.abstract: Abstract Masked diffusion language models using binary encoding and index shuffling achieve improved compute efficiency and performance over autoregressive models and previous masked diffusion approaches.  AI-generated summary Masked diffusion models (MDM) exhibit superior generalization when learned using a Partial masking scheme (Prime). This approach converts tokens into sub-tokens and models the diffusion process at the sub-token level. We identify two limitations of the MDM-Prime framework. First, we lack tools to guide the hyperparameter choice of the token granularity in the subtokenizer. Second, we find that the function form of the subtokenizer significantly degrades likelihood estimation when paired with commonly used Byte-Pair-Encoding (BPE) tokenizers. To address these limitations, we study the tightness of the variational bound in MDM-Prime and develop MDM-Prime-v2, a masked diffusion language model which incorporates Binary Encoding and Index Shuffling. Our scaling analysis reveals that MDM-Prime-v2 is 21.8times more compute-efficient than autoregressive models (ARM). In compute-optimal comparisons, MDM-Prime-v2 achieves 7.77 perplexity on OpenWebText, outperforming ARM (12.99), MDM (18.94), and MDM-Prime (13.41). When extending the model size to 1.1B parameters, our model further demonstrates superior zero-shot accuracy on various commonsense reasoning tasks.

ru.title: Эффективная диффузионная языковая модель через двоичное кодирование и перемешивание индексов
ru.desc: В работе предлагается MDM-Prime-v2 — маскированная диффузионная языковая модель, которая использует двоичное кодирование и перемешивание индексов для повышения эффективности вычислений. Авторы исследуют вариационные границы маскированных диффузионных моделей и решают проблемы выбора гиперпараметров при работе с BPE-токенизаторами. Новый подход достигает 21.8 раза лучшей вычислительной эффективности по сравнению с авторегрессивными моделями и превосходит их по качеству на стандартных бенчмарках. На больших моделях (1.1B параметров) метод показывает превосходные результаты в задачах рассуждения здравого смысла.

en.title: Revolutionizing Language Models with Efficient Masked Diffusion
en.desc: This paper introduces a new approach to masked diffusion language models (MDM) that enhances efficiency and performance compared to traditional autoregressive models. The authors propose MDM-Prime-v2, which utilizes binary encoding and index shuffling to improve the learning process at the sub-token level. They identify limitations in the previous MDM-Prime framework, particularly regarding hyperparameter selection and the impact of tokenization methods on likelihood estimation. The results show that MDM-Prime-v2 is significantly more compute-efficient and achieves better performance on language tasks, demonstrating its effectiveness in zero-shot scenarios.

zh.title: 掩蔽扩散模型的高效新突破
zh.desc: 这篇论文介绍了一种新的掩蔽扩散语言模型（MDM-Prime-v2），它通过二进制编码和索引洗牌来提高计算效率和性能。该模型在部分掩蔽方案下学习，能够在子标记级别建模扩散过程，从而展现出更好的泛化能力。研究中指出了MDM-Prime框架的两个局限性，并提出了改进方案。最终，MDM-Prime-v2在计算效率上比自回归模型高出21.8倍，并在多个常识推理任务中表现出色。

papers.title: Residual Stream Duality in Modern Transformer Architectures
papers.abstract: Abstract The residual stream in Transformers can be viewed through a two-axis framework where sequence position and layer depth provide different pathways for information flow, with causal depth-wise residual attention equivalent to sequence-axis short sliding-window attention.  AI-generated summary Recent work has made clear that the residual pathway is not mere optimization plumbing; it is part of the model's representational machinery. We agree, but argue that the cleanest way to organize this design space is through a two-axis view of the Transformer. A decoder evolves information along two ordered dimensions: sequence position and layer depth. Self-attention already provides adaptive mixing along the sequence axis, whereas the residual stream usually performs fixed addition along the depth axis. If we fix a token position and treat layer index as the ordered variable, then a causal depth-wise residual attention read is exactly the same local operator as causal short sliding-window attention (ShortSWA), except written over depth rather than over sequence. This is the core residual stream duality behind Transformer^2. This perspective also clarifies the recent literature. ELC-BERT and DenseFormer already show that learned aggregation over depth can outperform uniform residual accumulation, while Vertical Attention, DeepCrossAttention (DCA), MUDDFormer, and Attention Residuals move further toward explicit attention-based routing over earlier layers. The key point, however, is that operator-level duality does not imply systems-level symmetry. For large-scale autoregressive models, sequence-axis ShortSWA is usually the more hardware-friendly placement because it reuses token-side sliding-window kernels, KV-cache layouts, and chunked execution. If the goal is instead to change the shortcut itself, Deep Delta Learning (DDL) is the cleaner intervention because it modifies the residual operator directly rather than adding a separate cross-layer retrieval path. Our recommendation is therefore simple: use DDL when the shortcut is the object of interest, and use sequence-axis ShortSWA when the goal is local adaptive mixing.

ru.title: Трансформер как двумерный поток: дуальность глубины и последовательности
ru.desc: В статье предлагается двухосевая框架для анализа остаточного потока в Трансформерах, где информация течет вдоль двух измерений: позиции в последовательности и глубины слоя. Авторы показывают, что причинное внимание вдоль оси глубины математически эквивалентно скользящему окну внимания по оси последовательности, но применённому между слоями. На основе этой дуальности предложены рекомендации: использовать Deep Delta Learning для модификации самого остаточного соединения или ShortSWA для локального адаптивного смешивания информации. Такая перспектива объединяет и систематизирует недавние работы по улучшению архитектуры Трансформеров через изучение агрегации информации на разных уровнях сети.

en.title: Transforming Residuals: A Dual Pathway for Information Flow in Transformers
en.desc: This paper explores the role of the residual stream in Transformer models, proposing a two-axis framework that considers both sequence position and layer depth for information flow. It highlights that the residual pathway is integral to the model's representation, rather than just a means for optimization. The authors demonstrate that causal depth-wise residual attention can be viewed as a local operator similar to causal short sliding-window attention, emphasizing the duality in the residual stream. They recommend using Deep Delta Learning for modifying shortcuts and sequence-axis ShortSWA for adaptive mixing, depending on the specific goals of the model design.

zh.title: 变换器的残差流：双轴视角下的信息流动
zh.desc: 本文探讨了变换器中的残差流，通过两个轴的框架来理解信息流动的不同路径：序列位置和层深度。我们认为，残差路径不仅仅是优化的工具，而是模型表示机制的一部分。自注意力机制在序列轴上提供了自适应混合，而残差流通常在深度轴上执行固定的加法。我们建议在关注快捷方式时使用深度增量学习（DDL），而在需要局部自适应混合时使用序列轴短滑动窗口注意力（ShortSWA）。

papers.title: CCTU: A Benchmark for Tool Use under Complex Constraints
papers.abstract: Abstract  Solving problems through tool use under explicit constraints constitutes a highly challenging yet unavoidable scenario for large language models (LLMs), requiring capabilities such as function calling, instruction following, and self-refinement. However, progress has been hindered by the absence of dedicated evaluations. To address this, we introduce CCTU, a benchmark for evaluating LLM tool use under complex constraints. CCTU is grounded in a taxonomy of 12 constraint categories spanning four dimensions (i.e., resource, behavior, toolset, and response). The benchmark comprises 200 carefully curated and challenging test cases across diverse tool-use scenarios, each involving an average of seven constraint types and an average prompt length exceeding 4,700 tokens. To enable reliable evaluation, we develop an executable constraint validation module that performs step-level validation and enforces compliance during multi-turn interactions between models and their environments. We evaluate nine state-of-the-art LLMs in both thinking and non-thinking modes. Results indicate that when strict adherence to all constraints is required, no model achieves a task completion rate above 20%. Further analysis reveals that models violate constraints in over 50% of cases, particularly in the resource and response dimensions. Moreover, LLMs demonstrate limited capacity for self-refinement even after receiving detailed feedback on constraint violations, highlighting a critical bottleneck in the development of robust tool-use agents. To facilitate future research, we release the data and code.
affiliations: College of Computer Science and Artificial Intelligence, Fudan University

ru.title: Когда LLM с инструментами не могут следовать правилам
ru.desc: В статье представлена CCTU — бенчмарк для оценки способности больших языковых моделей использовать инструменты при наличии сложных ограничений. Авторы разработали таксономию из 12 категорий ограничений по четырём измерениям (ресурсы, поведение, набор инструментов и формат ответа) и создали 200 тестовых примеров с средней длиной промпта более 4700 токенов. Для надёжной оценки реализован модуль валидации ограничений, который проверяет соответствие на каждом шаге взаимодействия между моделью и окружением. Результаты показывают, что при строгом соблюдении всех ограничений ни одна из девяти протестированных LLM не достигает 20% успешных завершений задач, что указывает на критические проблемы в разработке надёжных агентов с использованием инструментов.

en.title: CCTU: Benchmarking Tool Use Under Constraints for LLMs
en.desc: This paper addresses the challenges faced by large language models (LLMs) when using tools under strict constraints. It introduces CCTU, a benchmark designed to evaluate LLM performance in complex scenarios involving multiple constraints. The benchmark includes 200 test cases that require models to navigate various resource, behavior, toolset, and response constraints. The findings reveal that current LLMs struggle significantly with constraint adherence, achieving low task completion rates and frequently violating constraints, indicating a need for improved self-refinement capabilities.

zh.title: 评估大型语言模型工具使用能力的新基准
zh.desc: 本文介绍了CCTU，一个用于评估大型语言模型（LLMs）在复杂约束下工具使用能力的基准。CCTU基于12个约束类别的分类法，涵盖资源、行为、工具集和响应四个维度，包含200个精心策划的测试案例。研究表明，当要求严格遵守所有约束时，没有模型的任务完成率超过20%，且模型在超过50%的情况下违反约束，尤其是在资源和响应维度。此研究揭示了LLMs在自我改进能力方面的局限性，并为未来的研究提供了数据和代码。

papers.title: SuperLocalMemory V3: Information-Geometric Foundations for Zero-LLM Enterprise Agent Memory
papers.abstract: Abstract Mathematical foundations for AI agent memory are established through information-geometric, sheaf-theoretic, and stochastic-dynamical approaches, enabling improved retrieval, lifecycle management, and contradiction detection.  AI-generated summary Persistent memory is a central capability for AI agents, yet the mathematical foundations of memory retrieval, lifecycle management, and consistency remain unexplored. Current systems employ cosine similarity for retrieval, heuristic decay for salience, and provide no formal contradiction detection.   We establish information-geometric foundations through three contributions. First, a retrieval metric derived from the Fisher information structure of diagonal Gaussian families, satisfying Riemannian metric axioms, invariant under sufficient statistics, and computable in O(d) time. Second, memory lifecycle formulated as Riemannian Langevin dynamics with proven existence and uniqueness of the stationary distribution via the Fokker-Planck equation, replacing hand-tuned decay with principled convergence guarantees. Third, a cellular sheaf model where non-trivial first cohomology classes correspond precisely to irreconcilable contradictions across memory contexts.   On the LoCoMo benchmark, the mathematical layers yield +12.7 percentage points over engineering baselines across six conversations, reaching +19.9 pp on the most challenging dialogues. A four-channel retrieval architecture achieves 75% accuracy without cloud dependency. Cloud-augmented results reach 87.7%. A zero-LLM configuration satisfies EU AI Act data sovereignty requirements by architectural design. To our knowledge, this is the first work establishing information-geometric, sheaf-theoretic, and stochastic-dynamical foundations for AI agent memory systems.

ru.title: Математические основания памяти AI агентов через информационную геометрию и теорию пучков
ru.desc: В работе предложены строгие математические основы для памяти AI агентов, базирующиеся на информационной геометрии, теории пучков и стохастической динамике. Авторы разработали метрику поиска на основе структуры информации Фишера для диагональных гауссовских семейств, формализовали жизненный цикл памяти через динамику Ланжевена на римановом многообразии с гарантией сходимости и предложили модель клеточных пучков для обнаружения противоречий в памяти агента. Экспериментальные результаты на бенчмарке LoCoMo показывают улучшение на 12.7 процентных пункта над инженерными базовыми решениями, а четырёхканальная архитектура достигает 75% точности без облачной инфраструктуры.

en.title: Revolutionizing AI Memory: A Mathematical Approach
en.desc: This paper establishes the mathematical foundations for memory in AI agents using advanced concepts like information geometry, sheaf theory, and stochastic dynamics. It introduces a new retrieval metric based on Fisher information that enhances the efficiency of memory retrieval while ensuring consistency. The authors propose a Riemannian Langevin dynamics approach for memory lifecycle management, replacing traditional decay methods with a more principled framework. Additionally, they present a cellular sheaf model to detect contradictions in memory, significantly improving performance on benchmark tasks compared to existing systems.

zh.title: 建立人工智能代理记忆的数学基础
zh.desc: 本文建立了人工智能代理记忆的数学基础，采用信息几何、层叠理论和随机动力学的方法，提升了记忆检索、生命周期管理和矛盾检测的能力。当前的系统主要依赖余弦相似度进行检索，缺乏正式的矛盾检测机制。我们提出了一种基于Fisher信息结构的检索度量，确保了计算效率和一致性。通过Riemann Langevin动力学，我们为记忆生命周期提供了理论支持，并通过细胞层叠模型精确识别记忆上下文中的不可调和矛盾。

papers.title: ECG-Reasoning-Benchmark: A Benchmark for Evaluating Clinical Reasoning Capabilities in ECG Interpretation
papers.abstract: Abstract  While Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) show promising performance in automated electrocardiogram interpretation, it remains unclear whether they genuinely perform actual step-by-step reasoning or just rely on superficial visual cues. To investigate this, we introduce ECG-Reasoning-Benchmark, a novel multi-turn evaluation framework comprising over 6,400 samples to systematically assess step-by-step reasoning across 17 core ECG diagnoses. Our comprehensive evaluation of state-of-the-art models reveals a critical failure in executing multi-step logical deduction. Although models possess the medical knowledge to retrieve clinical criteria for a diagnosis, they exhibit near-zero success rates (6% Completion) in maintaining a complete reasoning chain, primarily failing to ground the corresponding ECG findings to the actual visual evidence in the ECG signal. These results demonstrate that current MLLMs bypass actual visual interpretation, exposing a critical flaw in existing training paradigms and underscoring the necessity for robust, reasoning-centric medical AI. The code and data are available at https://github.com/Jwoo5/ecg-reasoning-benchmark.

ru.title: Мультимодальные модели не способны проводить подлинное визуально-обоснованное медицинское рассуждение
ru.desc: В работе исследуется способность мультимодальных больших языковых моделей выполнять пошаговое логическое рассуждение при интерпретации электрокардиограмм. Авторы создали ECG-Reasoning-Benchmark — набор данных с более чем 6400 примерами для систематической оценки навыков многошагового вывода по 17 основным диагнозам ЭКГ. Результаты показали, что несмотря на наличие медицинских знаний, современные модели почти полностью не справляются с построением полной цепочки рассуждений и не связывают клинические критерии с реальными визуальными признаками на ЭКГ сигнале. Исследование выявило фундаментальный недостаток в обучении медицинских AI моделей и подчеркивает необходимость разработки более совершенных методов обучения, ориентированных на логическое рассуждение.

en.title: Unveiling the Reasoning Gap in ECG Interpretation with MLLMs
en.desc: This paper investigates the reasoning capabilities of Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) in interpreting electrocardiograms (ECGs). It introduces the ECG-Reasoning-Benchmark, a framework designed to evaluate the models' ability to perform step-by-step reasoning across various ECG diagnoses. The study finds that while these models can access medical knowledge, they struggle significantly with logical deduction, achieving only a 6% success rate in maintaining a complete reasoning chain. This highlights a major limitation in current MLLM training, indicating that they often rely on superficial cues rather than true visual interpretation of ECG signals.

zh.title: 推理能力不足的心电图解读模型
zh.desc: 这篇论文探讨了多模态大型语言模型（MLLMs）在自动心电图解读中的表现。研究引入了ECG-Reasoning-Benchmark，这是一个包含6400多个样本的多轮评估框架，用于系统评估心电图诊断中的逐步推理能力。评估结果显示，尽管模型具备医学知识，但在执行多步骤逻辑推理时表现不佳，成功率仅为6%。这表明当前的MLLMs在实际视觉解读方面存在严重缺陷，强调了需要更强大的以推理为中心的医疗人工智能。

papers.title: Theoretical Foundations of Latent Posterior Factors: Formal Guarantees for Multi-Evidence Reasoning
papers.abstract: Abstract Latent Posterior Factors provides a theoretical framework for combining heterogeneous evidence in probabilistic prediction tasks with formal guarantees for trustworthy AI applications.  AI-generated summary We present a complete theoretical characterization of Latent Posterior Factors (LPF), a principled framework for aggregating multiple heterogeneous evidence items in probabilistic prediction tasks. Multi-evidence reasoning arises pervasively in high-stakes domains including healthcare diagnosis, financial risk assessment, legal case analysis, and regulatory compliance, yet existing approaches either lack formal guarantees or fail to handle multi-evidence scenarios architecturally. LPF encodes each evidence item into a Gaussian latent posterior via a variational autoencoder, converting posteriors to soft factors through Monte Carlo marginalization, and aggregating factors via exact Sum-Product Network inference (LPF-SPN) or a learned neural aggregator (LPF-Learned).   We prove seven formal guarantees spanning the key desiderata for trustworthy AI: Calibration Preservation (ECE <= epsilon + C/sqrt(K_eff)); Monte Carlo Error decaying as O(1/sqrt(M)); a non-vacuous PAC-Bayes bound with train-test gap of 0.0085 at N=4200; operation within 1.12x of the information-theoretic lower bound; graceful degradation as O(epsilon*delta*sqrt(K)) under corruption, maintaining 88% performance with half of evidence adversarially replaced; O(1/sqrt(K)) calibration decay with R^2=0.849; and exact epistemic-aleatoric uncertainty decomposition with error below 0.002%. All theorems are empirically validated on controlled datasets spanning up to 4,200 training examples. Our theoretical framework establishes LPF as a foundation for trustworthy multi-evidence AI in safety-critical applications.

ru.title: Надежное объединение разнородных доказательств с формальными гарантиями
ru.desc: Работа представляет теоретическую основу для Latent Posterior Factors (LPF) — метода, который объединяет несколько разнородных источников информации при решении задач вероятностного предсказания. Авторы предлагают два подхода: LPF-SPN, использующий точный вывод в сумматорно-произведенных сетях, и LPF-Learned с нейросетевым агрегатором. Исследование доказывает семь формальных гарантий надежности AI, включая сохранение калибровки, контролируемые ошибки Монте-Карло и устойчивость к искажению данных. Все теоретические результаты подтверждены экспериментально на контролируемых датасетах, что делает этот подход пригодным для критичных по безопасности приложений в медицине и финансах.

en.title: Trustworthy AI through Latent Posterior Factors
en.desc: The paper introduces Latent Posterior Factors (LPF), a framework designed to effectively combine different types of evidence in probabilistic prediction tasks. It addresses the challenges of multi-evidence reasoning in critical fields like healthcare and finance, where existing methods often lack reliability. LPF utilizes variational autoencoders to encode evidence into Gaussian latent posteriors and employs Monte Carlo methods for aggregation, ensuring robust predictions. The authors provide formal guarantees for the framework's performance, demonstrating its potential for trustworthy AI applications in high-stakes environments.

zh.title: 潜在后验因子：可信赖的多证据AI基础
zh.desc: 本论文提出了潜在后验因子（LPF）的理论框架，用于在概率预测任务中结合异构证据。LPF通过变分自编码器将每个证据项编码为高斯潜在后验，并通过蒙特卡洛边际化将后验转换为软因子。该框架在医疗诊断、金融风险评估等高风险领域具有重要应用，提供了七个正式保证，确保可信赖的人工智能。通过实验证明，LPF在多证据场景下表现出色，能够有效处理不确定性和校准问题。

papers.title: I Know What I Don't Know: Latent Posterior Factor Models for Multi-Evidence Probabilistic Reasoning
papers.abstract: Abstract Latent Posterior Factors framework combines variational autoencoder latent posteriors with sum-product network inference to enable probabilistic reasoning over unstructured evidence while maintaining calibrated uncertainty estimates.  AI-generated summary Real-world decision-making, from tax compliance assessment to medical diagnosis, requires aggregating multiple noisy and potentially contradictory evidence sources. Existing approaches either lack explicit uncertainty quantification (neural aggregation methods) or rely on manually engineered discrete predicates (probabilistic logic frameworks), limiting scalability to unstructured data.   We introduce Latent Posterior Factors (LPF), a framework that transforms Variational Autoencoder (VAE) latent posteriors into soft likelihood factors for Sum-Product Network (SPN) inference, enabling tractable probabilistic reasoning over unstructured evidence while preserving calibrated uncertainty estimates. We instantiate LPF as LPF-SPN (structured factor-based inference) and LPF-Learned (end-to-end learned aggregation), enabling a principled comparison between explicit probabilistic reasoning and learned aggregation under a shared uncertainty representation.   Across eight domains (seven synthetic and the FEVER benchmark), LPF-SPN achieves high accuracy (up to 97.8%), low calibration error (ECE 1.4%), and strong probabilistic fit, substantially outperforming evidential deep learning, LLMs and graph-based baselines over 15 random seeds.   Contributions: (1) A framework bridging latent uncertainty representations with structured probabilistic reasoning. (2) Dual architectures enabling controlled comparison of reasoning paradigms. (3) Reproducible training methodology with seed selection. (4) Evaluation against EDL, BERT, R-GCN, and large language model baselines. (5) Cross-domain validation. (6) Formal guarantees in a companion paper.

ru.title: Объединение глубокого обучения и вероятностного вывода для надёжного агрегирования доказательств
ru.desc: Авторы предлагают фреймворк Latent Posterior Factors, который объединяет вариационные автокодировщики с сетями sum-product для вероятностного вывода над неструктурированными данными. Метод преобразует скрытые апостериорные распределения VAE в мягкие факторы правдоподобия для SPN, сохраняя при этом калибровированные оценки неопределённости. Фреймворк реализован в двух вариантах: LPF-SPN с явным вероятностным выводом и LPF-Learned с обучаемой агрегацией, что позволяет сравнивать оба подхода. Экспериментальная оценка на восьми датасетах показала высокую точность и низкую ошибку калибровки, превосходя методы evidential deep learning и графовые модели.

en.title: Bridging Uncertainty and Probabilistic Reasoning with LPF
en.desc: The Latent Posterior Factors (LPF) framework integrates variational autoencoders with sum-product networks to enhance probabilistic reasoning over unstructured data. It addresses the challenge of aggregating noisy evidence while providing calibrated uncertainty estimates, which is crucial for real-world decision-making.
